perturbation.original_text: Any trade secrets will be entitled to all of the protections and benefits of applicable trade secret laws and any other applicable laws. For avoidance of doubt, the parties acknowledge and agree that, in the event a court of competent jurisdiction determines that information the disclosing p arty deems to be a trade secret is not a trade secret under applicable law, such information will nevertheless be deemed to be Confidential Information for purposes hereof, provided such information falls within the description set forth in Section 1(a).
perturbation.changed_text: Any trade secrets will be entitled to only some of the protections and benefits of applicable trade secret laws. For avoidance of doubt, the parties acknowledge and agree that, in the event a court of competent jurisdiction determines that information the disclosing p arty deems to be a trade secret is not a trade secret under applicable law, such information will not be deemed to be Confidential Information for purposes hereof, and not fall within the description set forth in Section 1(a).
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation modifies the treatment of trade secrets. The original text ensures that even if something isn't legally a trade secret, it's still treated as Confidential Information. The changed text removes this protection, stating it will NOT be treated as Confidential Information if a court disagrees. This conflicts with Section 1(a) and reduces the scope of protection, and creates ambiguity regarding the definition of trade secrets.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The disclo sing party’s Confidential Information shall remain the property of the disclosing party . No license under any patent, trademark, copyright or other intellectual property or proprietary right is granted or implied to any information furnished by or on beha lf of either party to the other party, whether or not Confidential Information. Each party acknowledge s that the other party’s Confidential Information is highly valuable, confidential and proprietary to the other party and has been developed through the investment of significant time, effort and expense, and that maintaining the confidentiality of this Confidential Information is essential for the general successful operations of TTU and Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text ensured that even if something isn't legally a trade secret, it's still treated as Confidential Information, but the changed text removes this protection, stating it will NOT be treated as Confidential Information. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which states that the disclosing party’s Confidential Information shall remain the property of the disclosing party and is highly valuable, confidential and proprietary. The changed text reduces the scope of protection, and creates ambiguity regarding the definition of trade secrets that directly makes the changed text contradictory to the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, “Confidential Information” shall not mean information that, as demonstrate d by the receiving party , is (i) in the public domain prior to the date of this Agreement through no wrongful act of the receiving party or its agents or employees, (ii) independently developed by the receiving party without any use of, or reference to, Confidential Information, (iii) rightfully furnished to the receiving party by any third party without violation or breach of any confidentiality restriction, or (iv) authorized in writing for release by the disclosing party .
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, “Confidential Information” shall not mean information that, as demonstrate d by the receiving party , is (i) in the public domain prior to the date of this Agreement through no wrongful act of the receiving party or its agents or employees, (ii) independently developed by the receiving party with any use of, or reference to, Confidential Information, (iii) rightfully furnished to the receiving party by any third party without violation or breach of any confidentiality restriction, or (iv) authorized in writing for release by the disclosing party .
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation removes the 'without any use of, or reference to, Confidential Information' from exception (ii). The initial text excludes information that has been independently developed using confidential information of the disclosing party, from the definition of confidential information. The effect of this change will result in the protection of information even if it was derived from the use of the disclosing party's confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the Tennessee Public Records Act, Section 10 -7-5-1 et seq., a t all times during the term of this Agreement and thereafter, the receiving party shall: (i) refrain from disclosing , directly or indirectly, any Confidential Information of the disclosing party to third parties , and hold all Confidential Information received from the disclosing party in trust and strict confidence, subject to the terms of this Agreement ; (ii) take reasonable security precautions, at least as great as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care, to keep confidential the disclosing party’s Confidential Information and avoid d isclosure, loss or misuse of the Confidential Information; and (iii) refrain from using any of the disclosing party’s Confidential Information for any purpose not specified her eunder (including , without limitation , in its own business or for its own benefit) without the prior written consent of the disclosing p arty.
perturbation.justification: The change in the 'Changed Text' removes the phrase 'without any use of, or reference to, Confidential Information' from exception (ii) regarding the definition of 'Confidential Information'. This means that information developed by the receiving party, even if it uses the disclosing party's Confidential Information, might not be considered Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which states that the receiving party shall 'refrain from using any of the disclosing party’s Confidential Information for any purpose' without consent. The change allows for the use of confidential information in development without it being classified as such, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction regarding the protection and use of confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the Tennessee Public Records Act, Section 10 -7-5-1 et seq., a t all times during the term of this Agreement and thereafter, the receiving party shall: (i) refrain from disclosing , directly or indirectly, any Confidential Information of the disclosing party to third parties , and hold all Confidential Information received from the disclosing party in trust and strict confidence, subject to the terms of this Agreement ; (ii) take reasonable security precautions, at least as great as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care, to keep confidential the disclosing party’s Confidential Information and avoid d isclosure, loss or misuse of the Confidential Information; and (iii) refrain from using any of the disclosing party’s Confidential Information for any purpose not specified her eunder (including , without limitation , in its own business or for its own benefit) without the prior written consent of the disclosing p arty.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party is permitted to disclose Confidential Information of the disclosing party to third parties and is not required to hold such information in trust or strict confidence. The receiving party is not required to take any precautions to keep confidential the disclosing party's Confidential Information and may freely disclose, lose, or misuse it. The receiving party is permitted to use the disclosing party's Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever, including in its own business or for its own benefit, without the prior written consent of the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: The change completely reverses the obligations regarding Confidential Information, effectively negating the entire purpose of the NDA. The original text explicitly prohibits disclosure and misuse. This version allows it, directly contradicting the intent and creating an immediate and serious conflict within the contract, particularly with section 3. By providing an exception that reverses the confidentiality rules, uncertainty is introduced into the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The disclo sing party’s Confidential Information shall remain the property of the disclosing party . No license under any patent, trademark, copyright or other intellectual property or proprietary right is granted or implied to any information furnished by or on beha lf of either party to the other party, whether or not Confidential Information. Each party acknowledge s that the other party’s Confidential Information is highly valuable, confidential and proprietary to the other party and has been developed through the investment of significant time, effort and expense, and that maintaining the confidentiality of this Confidential Information is essential for the general successful operations of TTU and Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text by reversing the confidentiality obligations. The changed text explicitly allows disclosure and misuse of confidential information, while the contradicted text emphasizes the value and proprietary nature of the information, stating that maintaining confidentiality is essential. This creates a clear conflict and invalidates the purpose of the confidentiality clause.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall not be assignable by either party , in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of the other party ; provided, however, that a party may assign this Agreement without consent in the even t of a sale of securities or assets, merger or change of control of such party. Any assignment in violation of this Subsection 4(d) shall be null and void. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement shall be binding upon, inure to the benefit of, and be enforceable by and against the successors and pe rmitte d assigns of each party to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall not be assignable by either party , in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of the other party ; provided, however, that a party may not assign this Agreement without consent in the even t of a sale of securities or assets, merger or change of control of such party. Any assignment in violation of this Subsection 4(d) shall be null and void. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement shall be binding upon, inure to the benefit of, and be enforceable by and against the successors and pe rmitte d assigns of each party to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change makes it contradictory as to whether the agreement can be assigned in the event of a sale. The original statement says a party 'may assign' without consent, while the modified version says a party 'may not assign' without consent. The two statements create a contradiction as to whether assignment can occur at all without consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall not be assignable by either party , in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of the other party ; provided, however, that a party may not assign this Agreement without consent in the even t of a sale of securities or assets, merger or change of control of such party. Any assignment in violation of this Subsection 4(d) shall be null and void. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement shall be binding upon, inure to the benefit of, and be enforceable by and against the successors and pe rmitte d assigns of each party to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'may assign' with 'may not assign' in the exception regarding assignment during a sale, merger, or change of control. This directly contradicts the original clause, which previously allowed assignment without consent in such events. The contradicted text and the changed text are the same, making the contradiction stark and impactful. The change directly contradicts the terms of the contradicted text. This contradiction creates uncertainty about whether assignment is permitted, aligning with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement with or without cause upon ninety (90) days prior written notice to the other party. All sections of this Agreement relating to the rights and obligations Page 3 of 4 Page 4 of 4 of the parties concerning Confidential Information disclosed during the term of the Agreement shall survive any such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement with or without cause upon ninety (90) days prior written notice to the other party. No sections of this Agreement relating to the rights and obligations Page 3 of 4 Page 4 of 4 of the parties concerning Confidential Information disclosed during the term of the Agreement shall survive any such termination.
perturbation.explanation: The change removes the clause stating that confidentiality obligations survive termination. The original statement says that all sections of the agreement, relating to the rights and obligations survive termination, while the modified version says no sections relating to the rights and obligations shall survive termination. The two statements create a contradiction as to whether the agreement, will last after termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the Tennessee Public Records Act, Section 10 -7-5-1 et seq., a t all times during the term of this Agreement and thereafter, the receiving party shall: (i) refrain from disclosing , directly or indirectly, any Confidential Information of the disclosing party to third parties , and hold all Confidential Information received from the disclosing party in trust and strict confidence, subject to the terms of this Agreement ; (ii) take reasonable security precautions, at least as great as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care, to keep confidential the disclosing party’s Confidential Information and avoid d isclosure, loss or misuse of the Confidential Information; and (iii) refrain from using any of the disclosing party’s Confidential Information for any purpose not specified her eunder (including , without limitation , in its own business or for its own benefit) without the prior written consent of the disclosing p arty.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that confidentiality obligations survive termination, while the changed text states that no obligations survive termination. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which specifies confidentiality obligations that extend 'at all times during the term of this Agreement and thereafter.' The change introduces a direct conflict regarding whether confidentiality obligations continue after the agreement is terminated, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
